name: docker-helper
description: Docker Compose generation, optimization, and troubleshooting assistance.
capabilities: Generate Dockerfiles for Node.js, Python, and Go applications
Create docker-compose.yml files for multi-service architectures
Optimize image sizes using multi-stage builds and layer caching
Troubleshoot container startup, networking, and volume issues
Apply security best practices including non-root users and minimal images
Configure health checks and resource limits for production deployments
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only SKILL.md with Docker best practices and examples. All 57 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES: the reported 'shell commands' are Docker examples in code blocks, 'weak crypto' is misinterpreted build flags, and 'credential access' are example connection strings in documentation. No executable code, network calls, or system access exists.
valueStatement: Creating Docker configurations takes time and expertise. This skill provides ready-to-use templates, optimization techniques, and troubleshooting guidance for containerized applications.
limitations: Does not execute Docker commands on your system
Does not access or modify existing Docker configurations
Does not provide real-time container monitoring
Cannot deploy containers to cloud services directly

useCases.title: Containerize applications
useCases.description: Generate production-ready Dockerfiles for your applications with security and performance best practices.

useCases.title: Optimize build pipelines
useCases.description: Reduce build times and image sizes using multi-stage builds, caching strategies, and BuildKit optimizations.

useCases.title: Standardize configurations
useCases.description: Create consistent Docker standards across your team with documented best practices and templates.

promptTemplates.title: Generate Dockerfile
promptTemplates.scenario: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application
promptTemplates.prompt: @docker-helper Generate a multi-stage Dockerfile for a Node.js 18 application with TypeScript. Include production optimizations and security best practices.

promptTemplates.title: Create compose file
promptTemplates.prompt: @docker-helper Create a docker-compose.yml for a development environment with a React frontend, Node.js backend, and PostgreSQL database.

promptTemplates.title: Optimize existing setup
promptTemplates.prompt: @docker-helper Optimize this Docker setup for faster builds and smaller image size. Current base image is node:18, and build takes 5 minutes.

promptTemplates.title: Debug container issues
promptTemplates.prompt: @docker-helper My container exits immediately after starting with no error output. How do I troubleshoot this container startup issue?

bestPractices: Use multi-stage builds to separate build dependencies from runtime
Always run containers as non-root users to limit security risks
Implement health checks for critical services to enable proper orchestration
antiPatterns: Do not use 'latest' tag for base images in production
Do not store secrets or credentials in Dockerfiles or image layers
Do not run COPY . . early in Dockerfile as it invalidates cache on every change

faq.question: What programming languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: Python, Node.js, Go, Java, Ruby, and any language with a Dockerfile base image.

faq.question: What are the image size limits?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidance only. Actual limits depend on your Docker host and registry.

faq.question: Does this skill integrate with Docker Desktop?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates configurations that you apply using your own Docker installation.

faq.question: Is my code safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only generates text. Your code never leaves your local environment.

faq.question: Why is my container failing to start?
faq.answer: Common causes include missing dependencies, port conflicts, permission issues, or incorrect entrypoint commands.

faq.question: How is this different from Docker Compose CLI?
faq.answer: The skill generates configurations. The Docker Compose CLI executes them. Use both together for container workflows.
